name: Phyllo-Wrapped Brie with Carmelized Onions
recipeIngredient: 4 medium onions, cut into thin wedges
1/4 cup hazelnuts or walnuts, chopped and toasted
2 rounds brie or camembert (4-1/2 ounces each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 1 tablespoon of margarine in a large saucepan over medium-low heat.
2. Add onions and cook, covered, for about 15 minutes or until tender, stirring occasionally.
3. Sprinkle sugar over the onions and continue cooking, uncovered, for an additional 10-15 minutes or until browned, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in toasted nuts and allow to cool slightly.
5. Working with one phyllo sheet at a time, keep the remaining sheets covered with plastic wrap.
6. Lightly brush one sheet of dough with some melted margarine.
7. Place another sheet on top and brush with more margarine. Repeat with two more sheets, brushing each with margarine.
8. Cut a 12-inch circle from the stack and discard any trimmings.
9. Slice one round of brie in half horizontally.
10. Place the bottom half in the center of the phyllo stack.
11. Spread about 1 tablespoon of apricot spreadable fruit over the brie, then add about 1/4 of the onion-nut mixture.
12. Top with the other half of the brie and another portion of the onion-nut mixture.
13. Fold the phyllo up and over the filling, pleating as necessary and twisting to cover the top.
14. Brush the remaining phyllo with melted margarine.
15. Place one wrapped brie round in an 8 x 8 x 2 inch baking pan.
16. Chill for up to 24 hours before baking at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes or until golden.
17. Let stand for 5-10 minutes before serving.
